title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

what: npis: Vectorized NPIs for R — npis-package
title: npis: Vectorized NPIs for R — npis-package
text: S3 vctrs class representing National Provider Identifier, unique 10-digit identification number assigned healthcare providers United States. identifier crucial billing administrative transactions mandated Health Insurance Portability Accountability Act (HIPAA).

title: npis: Vectorized NPIs for R — npis-package
text: NPI must meet specifications detailed Final Rule Standard Unique Health Identifier Health Care Providers (69 FR 3434), published January 23, 2004. NPI must consist 9 numeric digits followed one numeric check digit. NPI must embedded intelligence. NPI format check digit calculation must compatible card issuer identifier standard health identification card. card standard developed NCITS, accredited ANSI. card issuer identifier used standard health identification card must unique. Therefore NPI must generated manner avoid collisions identifiers might used card issuer identifiers standard health identification cards. order avoid collisions, NPIs initially issued first digit 1 2. digits used first digits card issuer identifiers. Use first digits must coordinated use first digits standard health plan identifier, adopted. Within constraints . 4. , NPI generation must use scattering algorithm capability use possible numeric combinations beginning 1 2. NPI generated must unique, without requiring database access verification.

what: Requirements for the NPI Check Digit
title: npis: Vectorized NPIs for R — npis-package
text: NPI check digit calculated using Luhn algorithm computing modulus 10 "double-add-double" check digit, recognized ISO standard (ISO/IEC 7812). NPI used card issuer identifier standard health identification card, prefixed 80840, 80 indicates Health Applications 840 indicates United States. check digit always calculated prefix present. accomplished simply adding constant 24 check digit calculation NPI used without prefix.
